h1: Check Your Vocabulary Level by Words Known
description: Discover your vocabulary level by counting words you know. Free CEFR level calculator for English learners. No registration required.
intro: Learning a language takes hundreds of hours of focused exposure. According to the FSI, for English speakers: Romance languages ~600h, German ~1,100h, Arabic/Chinese ~2,200h. The key is consistency over intensity. Discover your approximate language level based on active vocabulary size. Data updated to 2026.
keyTakeaway: **Formula: words known × language difficulty** = your CEFR level. Based on established language learning research, this calculator instantly estimates your proficiency level from your active vocabulary count.
useCases: Track your vocabulary growth progress over time
Understand exactly where you stand on the CEFR scale
Set realistic goals for your next language proficiency level
Validate your self-assessment before taking official proficiency tests
Compare vocabulary requirements across different languages
explanation:
Learning a language is a sustained effort spanning hundreds of hours. Cognitive science (Ebbinghaus, spaced repetition) combined with immersion and active practice form the foundation established by FSI and Cambridge research.

## How It Works

This calculator estimates your CEFR language level based on the number of active vocabulary words you know. Enter the count of words you can use in conversation, and get an instant level assessment with your next target.

The calculator applies the formula with your input values and alerts you if numbers fall outside typical ranges.

## Important Notes

This calculator is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (professional certifications, academic progress), consult with a qualified language instructor. Data updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: CEFR (Common European Framework of Reference) is the standard scale for language proficiency: A1-A2 (beginner), B1-B2 (intermediate), C1-C2 (advanced). Each level requires more vocabulary and complex usage.

faq.q: How many words do you need to be fluent?
faq.a: B2 level (fluent for most situations) requires 4,000-5,000 active words. True fluency (C1-C2) needs 8,000-20,000+ words depending on the language and field.
A1 (beginner): 500-1,000 active words. You can introduce yourself, ask simple questions, and understand basic written English.
B1 (intermediate): 2,500-3,500 active words. You can handle everyday conversations, understand main points of clear text, and write simple messages.

faq.q: What is passive vs. active vocabulary?
faq.a: Passive vocabulary: words you recognize and understand (typically 2-4x your active words). Active vocabulary: words you use when speaking or writing.

faq.q: How do I count my vocabulary size?
faq.a: Estimate conservatively: count only words you're confident using in conversation or writing. Use online vocabulary tests for more accuracy (many are free).

faq.q: How long to go from A1 to B2?
faq.a: For English speakers learning most European languages: 600-1,200 hours of study. Intensive: 3-6 months. Part-time: 1-3 years depending on consistency.
Yes, all Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: What's the difference between vocabulary tests?
faq.a: Official CEFR tests (Cambridge, TOEFL) validate your level formally. Self-assessment tools like this give you a quick estimate. For certification, take a standardized test.
